channel.title: Live Orthodox
channel.description: Essays answering common Bible contradictions and working through the metaphysical questions behind Christian belief.

item.title: The Witnesses to the Resurrection: What the Evidence Can and Can't Show
item.description: Paul says 500 people saw the risen Jesus at once. No source outside the Bible confirms it. Here's an honest look at what that objection does and doesn't prove.

item.title: Can Morality Exist Without God
item.description: Plenty of atheists live good, moral lives, that was never really the question. The harder question is what grounds objective moral obligation in the first place.

item.title: Did Jesus Ever Actually Claim to Be God
item.description: He never says the exact words "I am God" in the Gospels. The claim shows up in other ways, and the people around him clearly understood it.

item.title: Is the God of the Old Testament Different from the God of the New Testament
item.description: Wrath and judgment on one side, love and mercy on the other. The contrast is real, but it isn't the split it first appears to be.

item.title: Is the Trinity Three Gods?
item.description: Father, Son, and Spirit, each called God, yet Scripture insists there is only one. The objection treats person and being as the same thing. They are not.

item.title: Who Incited David to Take the Census, God or Satan
item.description: 2 Samuel says the Lord moved David to count Israel. Chronicles says Satan did. Both books can be right at once.

item.title: How Many Women Were at the Tomb
item.description: Matthew names two, Mark names three, Luke leaves it open, John names one. This is exactly what independent eyewitness testimony looks like.

item.title: Does God Change His Mind
item.description: Numbers says God does not change his mind like a man. Genesis says he regretted making humanity. Both are true.

item.title: Can God's Existence Be Proven? The Argument from Contingency
item.description: Not a proof in the mathematical sense, but a serious argument that the universe cannot be its own explanation.

item.title: Essence and Energies: How Orthodoxy Reads the Nature of God
item.description: The Palamite distinction is where a lot of East and West disagreement actually starts. Here is what it is and why it matters.

item.title: Free Will and Divine Foreknowledge
item.description: If God already knows what you will choose tomorrow, is the choice actually yours. A distinction most debates skip past too quickly.

item.title: The Two Genealogies of Christ
item.description: Matthew and Luke trace different fathers back from Joseph. First century genealogical convention explains why.

item.title: Judas' Death: Hanged, or Fell Headlong
item.description: Matthew says Judas hanged himself. Acts describes him falling and bursting open. Both can be true, and probably are.

item.title: Two Creation Accounts: Genesis 1 and Genesis 2
item.description: Skeptics point to two orders of creation in the opening chapters of Genesis as proof of an editorial mistake. The genres involved say otherwise.

item.title: The Problem of Evil
item.description: If God is good and all powerful, why does suffering exist. The strongest form of the objection, and where it actually breaks down.
